title: agent-core: streaming responses + multi-turn conversation history
summary: Issue #575 reports two production-blocking gaps in packages/agent-core/src/session.ts: prompt() waits for a full provider response and emits one text_delta, and each prompt rebuilds messages from scratch instead of preserving multi-turn conversation context.
issueFindings: OpenAI/Azure and Anthropic calls are currently non-streaming fetch calls followed by response.json().
prompt() emits session_start, one text_delta containing the full response, then session_end.
messages are rebuilt inside each prompt() call from system prompt plus the current user prompt only.
There is no persisted user/assistant history, no public getHistory()/clearHistory() control, and no deterministic trimming policy.
packages/agent-core/src/session.test.ts currently confirms the second prompt request only includes the new user message.
Queued follow-up text via steer()/followUp() must continue to apply to the next prompt exactly once.
This blocks #582 and is related to #578, #587, #594, and #257, but transport-mux streaming fixes are out of scope unless an agent-core integration boundary requires a small shared extraction.
reuseAuditFindings: packages/transport-mux/src/engines/openai.ts already parses OpenAI/Azure SSE data lines, [DONE], content deltas, finish reasons, and usage.
packages/transport-mux/src/engines/anthropic.ts already parses Anthropic message stream events including content_block_delta text_delta and message_delta usage.
transport-mux tests provide realistic OpenAI chat and Anthropic stream fixtures that can inform agent-core tests without changing transport-mux behavior.
packages/agent-core/src/context/token-estimator.ts and packages/agent-platform/src/compression/compaction.ts provide existing rough token-estimation patterns for deterministic trimming decisions.
packages/agent-core/README.md already documents prompt() as streaming events and abort()/dispose() behavior that the current implementation does not fully satisfy.
packages/agent-platform/src/harness/invoker.ts creates and disposes an agent-core session for the direct agent-core harness path and must remain compatible.
acceptanceFocus: OpenAI/Azure chat completions are requested with stream: true and SSE chunks emit multiple incremental text_delta events as they arrive.
Anthropic messages are requested with stream: true and message streaming chunks emit equivalent normalized text_delta events.
AgentCorePromptResult.output remains the full concatenated assistant text with existing success, duration, and exitCode semantics.
Event ordering remains session_start, zero or more text_delta events, session_end on success, and error on failure.
A second prompt includes prior successful user/assistant turns plus the new user prompt.
Successful assistant output is appended to history only after completion; failed, timed-out, or aborted prompts do not corrupt future history with partial assistant text.
Public getHistory()/clearHistory() or equivalent controls exist, are typed, return defensive copies, and are documented.
History growth is bounded by deterministic turn and token-aware trimming behavior with focused tests.
followUp()/steer() behavior remains compatible and queued text is persisted into history exactly once.
Downstream agent-platform direct agent-core harness tests remain compatible.
nonGoals: Do not implement structured output, vision, abort enhancements, or token accounting beyond what streaming/history require.
Do not redesign agent-platform session management; #594 covers broader platform session management.
Do not fix transport-mux issue #257 unless a small shared parser extraction is required by agent-core tests.
Do not add shell-oriented process subtasks for this repository workflow.
qualityGates: Regression tests fail against the pre-fix behavior or clearly encode the prior broken expectation.
OpenAI/Azure streaming tests cover split SSE frames, multiple data chunks, [DONE], final output concatenation, request body stream: true, and event ordering.
Anthropic streaming tests cover content_block_delta text deltas, message_delta/message_stop completion behavior, final output concatenation, and event ordering.
Multi-turn tests cover prior user/assistant history inclusion on the second prompt.
History API tests cover getHistory(), clearHistory(), defensive copying, system prompt handling, trimming, and failed prompt behavior.
followUp()/steer() tests cover queued instruction persistence exactly once.
Relevant agent-platform direct harness tests pass if public types or session behavior changed.
Compatibility review finds no event-contract regression or unbounded-history risk.
verificationCommands: npm exec --yes --package=vitest -- vitest run --config packages/agent-core/vitest.config.ts packages/agent-core/src/session.test.ts

plannedPhases.name: Issue context
plannedPhases.purpose: Read #575, comments, labels, related issues, docs/agent-layer-gaps.md, and agent-core README session API documentation.

plannedPhases.name: Reuse audit
plannedPhases.purpose: Scan existing streaming parsers, fixtures, history/token helpers, public API boundaries, and old behavior tests before adding new infrastructure.

plannedPhases.name: Architecture trace
plannedPhases.purpose: Map prompt(), provider request shaping, event emission, queued follow-ups, history semantics, failure behavior, and downstream agent-platform integration.

plannedPhases.name: Regression plan
plannedPhases.purpose: Add failing tests for provider streaming, multi-turn history, history controls, trimming, follow-up persistence, and failure behavior before implementation.

plannedPhases.name: Implementation loop
plannedPhases.purpose: Implement isolated provider streaming parsers and persistent bounded history in agent-core, iterating up to three times based on verification and review.

plannedPhases.name: Verification gate
plannedPhases.purpose: Run targeted session tests, package tests, build, relevant downstream tests, and diff hygiene checks.

plannedPhases.name: Compatibility review
plannedPhases.purpose: Review event contracts, provider parser robustness, history semantics, public API typing, README consistency, and downstream agent-platform compatibility.

plannedPhases.name: Final acceptance
plannedPhases.purpose: Confirm acceptance criteria, quality gates, changed files, release-note candidate, and residual risks.

breakpoints.when: Only if implementation uncovers a real public API conflict, such as whether history should include failed user turns or whether clearHistory() should preserve the current sessionId.
